Welcome back to sell with story the brand-boosting blueprint that turns customers into heroes and clicks into dollars. We're so excited to jump in our last couple sections before we get to some really good thorough Q&A and some website review. Over to you Donald. Alright thanks so much Bethany. Well you've already got two really three of your pieces of marketing collateral that we wanted to create today. Your clarified message, your brand script script, your one-liner and your landing page. And so we want to go to the next one that's number 18 or page 18. That's right a lead generating PDF. So on that landing page people are moving from curiosity into enlightenment that second phase in the relationship. And once they do that you want to be able to capture their email address so that you can continue to nurture that relationship. There's a real reason that you want to capture that email address and that's because relationships take time to build. So when I moved to I moved to Washington DC to pursue my wife and we got married about eight years ago at about nine years ago or ten years ago. I moved to Washington DC from Portland Oregon and Betsy and I continued to date. We were dating long distance and and I moved out there. And at one point she sat me down. I'll never forget it was a hilarious conversation. She sat me down and she said, Don you know I'm so glad you moved to Washington DC and I love having you here. And I got to tell you though you are a you are a quality time guy and you are able to connect with people very quickly. You know what you want. You've never you know wonder what you want. And I thought she was complimenting me and she said she said well I got to tell you I'm a quantity time girl. That means you're going to need to be here for a while and I'm going to need to see you interact in a bunch of situations and know whether or not you're a goofball or not and that sometimes takes time. So I extended my short lease on my place across from the Supreme Court and continued to date Betsy and thank God she said yes and now we've been married living in Nashville for about eight years. I only tell that story because later as I was looking at my company and how we were doing business I noticed that up to 85% of people who bought from me had already gone through the sale cycle and now we're just in an email sequence where we were all we were doing was telling them who was going to be on the podcast. They had gone from a sale sequence to a nurture sequence and 85% of them if we would have not continued to email them we would have not bought my product. I want to make that really clear. If we hadn't continued to email our customers week after week after week my company would be 85% smaller than it is now. Right? Just do the math. What was my customer telling me? My customer was telling me Don we are quantity time people. We are not quality time people. It's going to take a while to earn trust and decide whether or not we want to move forward into a commitment. So in order to reach out to people and be a quantity time brand you got to have their email address and the only way to get their email address is with a lead generator that makes them want it's so valuable they want to give you their email address. So you got a page here, page 18 that will guide you through some questions that will help you create a good lead generator. But I'll just tell you the ones that we've used. The first lead generator that we used was called 5 Things Your Website Should Include. That was it. So you got like 40,000 people downloading that and my company was off to the races. Then we did 5 minute marketing makeover. If you went to 5minutemarketingmakeover.com you saw me I think it was 3, 5 minute videos that helped you figure out your marketing and that worked really well. And then on and on and on our podcast all sorts of different stuff but it's really it's the lead generators that built our list that helped us grow the company. So I'd love to take questions about lead generators when we get to the Q&A here in a second but you know it's a big project. I'll also say this. A lead generating PDF works about as well for us as video but video is much harder and more expensive to produce. So if you don't have a lead generator, just create a lead generating PDF and start capturing those email addresses right away. Another thing that your lead generator should do is it should make it known that you solve a problem. So 5 Things Your Website Should Include made it known that we solve the problem of messaging on your website. Business Made Simple was another I released a 5 minute or 3 to 5 minute business tip every day 5 days a week for a year. And we had 100,000 people sign up for that. And then we were able to turn that into Business Made Simple the book and Business Made Simple the 60 videos and Business Made Simple University. So you know there's all sorts of lead generators that you can do but you want that lead generator to begin to brand yourself as the person who solves a certain problem. That's the big thing with that lead generator. Get an email address and make it be known and enlighten people about how you solve a certain problem. So I've got in the notebook, in the workbook, I've got sample lead, a sample lead generator for you and a chance for you on page 22 to come up with a title for that lead generator and then go ahead and write it out. That's going to make you can also hire writers to do that. I know the digital market has all sorts of resources on that stuff. But if you're just hanging out and you haven't made a lead generator yet, go make one by the end of the year. Have a lead generator created and begin to collect email addresses. All right. The next task, task number four, the final task of our creating our our sales funnel is that email campaign. And there are two different types of email campaigns. There's the sales campaign and the nurturing campaign. If you're just going to create one, go ahead and create a nurturing campaign and put a P.S. at the bottom of every one of them that asks people to go ahead and place an order, right? But a nurturing campaign is basically an excuse to email your entire list at least once a week. I think twice a week, but at least once a week, you want to be in touch with your audience. What you're doing when you email them once a week is you're buying real estate in their brain. They need to see you once a week. You know, when I was in my 20s, I accidentally became the president of a publishing company, if you can believe it. I started in the warehouse and a series of people resigned or whatever or moved around. And I found myself within two years in the presidency. And it was an absentee owner. This is a small publishing house. It's an absentee owner, really wasn't paying a lot of attention to the company. And all of a sudden, the numbers started looking really amazing. And he hired a consultant to try to figure out why his company was growing, because he's got some Yahoo 20-year-olds in the driver's seat. And it couldn't be that guy. And it's got to be some of the reason, what do we need to do about this? And the consultant came back and said, no, it's Don. It's what Don is doing. And of course, I don't know anything about business. So I'm like, what am I doing? And I said, well, all I'm doing is there's this whole program called FileMaker Pro. And with FileMaker Pro, I was able to figure out pretty quickly where our sales were coming from. Turns out our sales were coming from 20% of our audience. This was pre-email marketing. And I actually was sending a piece of snail mail, physical direct mail to about 200, 250 customers, 20% of our buying audience. These were bookstores. Remember bookstores? They were buildings that had books in them. And so they were buying these books from us. And the owner of the company said, you got to be kidding me. I actually am on that list and I read that letter. There's no way those letters are building this company. Like your last letter literally talked about how you went camping. And I said, well, when you send a letter every week, you know, I run out of things to say. So I just told them I went camping. And I said, but Greg, you're missing the point. They're not actually reading the letter. That letter is going across their desk and it's going into the trash can. And that's why this company is growing. And he was just like, what in the world are you talking about? I said, Greg, every week we're just reminding them that we exist. That's it. Every week we're reminding them that we exist. Fast forward 20 years. Well, now you can actually email somebody and on the most intimate device in their life, their phone. Their phone is where their spouse text messages them. It's where their sports scores, where they find out if the Seahawks won or lost last night. It's where, you know, it's the most intimate device they have. And you are getting into their email box once or twice a week. And you know what they're doing? They're swiping it and deleting that email without reading it. And unless they're opening the email and unsubscribing, I'm telling you, swiping and deleting that email is unbelievably valuable for you. Because once a week it's reminding them that you exist. And when they have the problem that you solve when it when that window opens up in their life and they finally have the problem you solve, if you haven't been in their email box that week, you run the risk of them not remembering you and going to Google and finding a solution. So even if they're not opening your email, find a reason to email them every week. For us, we have a weekly podcast called the Building a Story Brand Podcast. We just had Seth Godin on. We're having Charles Coke on next week. It's a great podcast if you haven't subscribed to it. For us, the only reason I have a podcast is not to have a podcast. It's so that I can email thousands of people and tell them who's on so they don't forget about us. They see the name of the company. They see the logo. There may be a PS that says, hey, we've got a workshop coming up or whatever, a new course in the online platform. You know, there's just an excuse to email them. So you want to start emailing people and you want to find a reason to do it. Now, what does it mean? Here's where it gets really hard because people are like, what's the right email to send? There is no right email to send. You want to send a courteous, hopefully interesting email that solves a problem, that takes them through a paradigm shift, that overcomes a piece of resistance, or that just shares a tip. So let's say you run a grocery store. If I ran a grocery store, every week I'd send to my list a recipe of some sort of ingredient of things that they can buy at the grocery store. If I run a restaurant, I want to send out cocktail recipes. I want to send out behind-the-scenes footage of how you actually cook a steak, right? I want to send a video about why customer service is so important to us. Be careful not to make it too much about you and always make it something that serves the customer. But I'm going to find an excuse every week to email that list. And every so often I'm going to not do the nurture email and I'm going to put in a sales email. The difference in a nurture email, nurture email is great content that you're sharing that solves one of your customers problems and you're really not asking for anything in return. A sales email is from the very first sentence to the very last sentence you're selling something. It's your shark tank pitch. I want you to buy my product from the very first sentence and here's why because it solves this problem. Here's another reason because if you don't buy it, this bonus is going to go away. You are selling your customer in that sales email. In your workbook, I actually include a ton of sample emails. Not a ton. I mean, if you wait, it's probably not a ton. But there's probably four or five sample emails for circuit e-bikes. I also give you formulas. Look at page 38. It's page 38. The subject line of your email, of your sales letter, this is the close the sale email, is what? Character. Let's just fill this out. If my demographic is 60 and older maybe. Remember when you used to love bikes but now it's too hard to get that bike up the hill. The right problem. And it's making you feel like you're missing out on the great outdoors. You should never be too old to ride a bike. Emotional philosophical struggle. Basically, you take your brand script and your brand script helps you write word for word a great sales letter that will work. In fact, if some of you guys are struggling a little bit maybe because of COVID financially and you need to get some cash flow in, use that page, these multiple pages, write a good sales letter and email it to your list and get some cash in the door. It should work for you. It's been working for 2,500 years. It should continue to work. It's just inviting your customer into a story. So how do I, what am I, you know, and again digital marketer is so much better at this than we are. So I feel like I'm visiting the Seahawks and teaching them how to play football. You need to run faster. Anyway, no, but I will say, you know, we use these combinations of nurture campaigns and sales campaigns. Nurture campaign lets people know who's on the podcast. If you open three or four of those in a row, I'm probably going to take you into a sales campaign. And the sales campaign tries to close the deal on whatever it is that we offer. Okay? So, and we have different lead generators for different products. And if you, you know, I worked with a financial advisor not long ago, they have, you know, he said, Don, there's basically three customers for a financial advisor. There's new money. There is, you know, existing money, say, five million or so that that person has. And then there's 10 or 15 million. And those customers tend to be much older. So we came up with three lead generators. The first lead generator is five mistakes people make with their first million. Well, who would download that? Somebody who is younger and they just made their first million. So after you make your first million, you really only care about making more until you get to about five million. And then you realize, Oh, my word, I pay enormous amounts of money in what taxes? So the second lead generator is five tax loopholes most people don't know about. Now, if you download that and you're thinking about saving money on taxes, you probably have seven figures sitting somewhere in the bank. I don't know. People don't hate people don't hate taxes until they have money. And they start hating taxes. And then the last one is the lead generator we came up with is how to leave your your children your inheritance without messing them up. Because most people, most trust fund kids, you know, struggle with unique problems because they haven't they haven't had to earn that money. And so you've got to kind of go around this idea of like money actually costs a lot of work and you don't realize that or they might spend it all or something. So what so if you downloaded the how to leave money to your kids without screwing them up, we know that you are 65 years old and you have five million dollars in the bank and whatever. If you download the tax loophole lead generator, we know that you probably have a company that company starting to make pretty good money. So we're going to on ramp you with different sales sequences. If you downloaded five mistakes people make with their first million, we know that you're probably younger and you've got a business that's starting to do well. And we're going to on ramp you differently. Those would be three different brand scripts and we're going to create nurturing and sales campaigns for those three demographics. It's a little bit more advanced right now for most of you. I would say simply get a good lead generator and start emailing people. Put a P.S. at the bottom reminding them of other products that you sell and your company is going to grow. You know, here's what we found mid March. We've been telling people about this stuff for five years now and we've been helping them create sales funnels and all that kind of stuff. And what we found mid March when the whole economy shut down is that almost no matter what your company did you could survive if you had a sales funnel. If you had not created a sales funnel when we told you to you were probably toast because everybody, including us, had to email their customers and pivot their message. And if you didn't have email addresses and had not earned trust by enlightening customers and peaking their curiosity, you were not going to make it. And I saw a United Friend who did everything that we told him to do. He's a plastic surgeon in Amarillo, Texas. A plastic surgeon is not going to make a lot of money during quarantine, right? But he had this huge list in Amarillo and he emailed everybody and said, Hey, you're probably out two weeks into quarantine. He said, You're sick of watching Netflix. There's nothing on television. Why don't we do a Zoom call with one of our doctors and put together a plan for when we come out of quarantine that you can look and feel your best. It's a 15 minute call. Not going to cost you anything. And he just lined up hundreds of calls and was able to every doctor was able to say, Well, here's what we do. Here's what you do. You know, you can have a better smile. We can do this or that. And now it's business coming out of quarantine is skyrocketing. His competitor, bank grant out of business. Okay, so that's a tough one. Let's go even tougher. Fine dining restaurant in Seattle, Washington, canless restaurant. If you've ever eaten at canless, it's a wonderful restaurant and Mark and Brian are friends and story brand alumni. How in the world is a fine dining restaurant going to survive a pandemic? You can't even get people into the kitchen much less the dining room. But they done what we told him to do. They had and a fine dining restaurant collecting email addresses. I mean, you know, most fine dining people would say, No, we don't market ourselves at all. There's a line out the door. It's hard to get a reservation. We are never going to ask you to come here. But these guys are way more humble than that. By the way, they wanted James Beard Award last year. And I think they want a Michelin star. I mean, this is a serious restaurant. They've won grand award on their wine list for 10 years in a row. They're dead at the pandemic, right? Not so. They immediately got their staff together and say, Hey, what does this make possible? The chef said, You know, I make a really great hamburger, a really good one. And people think I'm a fine dining chef and I want a Michelin star and I want a James Beard. Wait till you taste my hamburger. And they turned the valet stand into a burger stand. And that the police had to literally come and surround the restaurant in direct traffic because so many people wanted the canless burger. That does not happen unless you have an email list. You cannot pivot. Then they went from there and they did drive in movies in their they want their goal was they had 120 staff members and they said, We will not fire any of you. Period. You will all make money. And they started a drive in movie theater in the parking lot at canless where you could come and watch a movie. Then they started a CSA delivery. They started a cocktail service where they take people you could literally at one point get a family meal from canless delivered to your house. And then they piped in the piano player from the canless dining room and you could put them on your big screen TV and eat canless for the first time ever at home watching the canless piano player. None of that is possible. Now they have no kidding because they're committed. They are not firing their team members, but the restaurant has been closed for six months. What do you do? They just started canless community college. So for I think 25 bucks you remember and then you pay for courses, field trips, anything you know that you learn about wine. You can learn about all this kind of stuff. They've completely gone digital. They are still what is it six months in there still they still have every single one of their people are still employed. Now here's what's even more amazing. They've given away $250,000 to other restaurants and farmers in the area who are suffering. It's just amazing. That happens when you have a sales funnel that collects email addresses and you have a loving close relationship with that email list because they know you are so valuable. All right. So create a sales funnel. Here's a challenge for you. What if you had every aspect of your sales funnel created by the end of the year? And what if you could look back on 2020 and say this, were it not for the COVID pandemic 2021 would not have been the greatest year in the history of my company? That's the goal, right? That's the climactic scene. This story is going to head toward. You're going to take your clear message. You're going to put it into marketing collateral. You're going to execute that collateral. You do not get points for fumbling on the one yard line only for putting the ball in the end zone. If this doesn't become a website, you won't make money. If this doesn't become an email, you won't make money. If this doesn't become a one liner on the back of your business card, you will not make money. You do not make money from ideas. You make money from executing ideas. So make sure that you execute and create your sales funnel. Okay.
